name: haproxy

command: set -eo pipefail; echo 'show info' | socat -t 5 - UNIX-CONNECT:/var/run/haproxy/admin.sock | grep -E '^Version:'

note: GPL-2.0+. Stock haproxy:2.9-alpine used as a TCP/SNI router on :443 (R1-1 / R1-2 in 2026-05-04 audit; landed in v0.0.33). Sniffs the SNI from each TLS ClientHello WITHOUT decrypting and TCP-forwards raw bytes to either caddy:8444 (panel subdomain) or sing-box:443 (proxy domain — NaiveProxy). No TLS termination here, so the on-the-wire fingerprint stays whatever the backend negotiates with the client (anti-tracking probe-resistance preserved). Verify probe is the Cycle 2 / 5 drift-detection upgrade (v0.0.43) — sends `show info` over the haproxy stats socket via socat (UNIX-domain only, never reachable over the network), greps the `Version:` line, and the matcher's soft version match asserts the pinned `version` field above is contained in that line. Socket is opened by haproxy.cfg.tpl's `stats socket /var/run/haproxy/admin.sock mode 666 level user` directive (level user is read-only — `show *` allowed, runtime state mutation forbidden). The panel container mounts the haproxy_admin volume RO; the volume is the security boundary, not the file mode. Mode is 666 (v0.0.52 fix) because the Filament Components page invokes the probe via php-fpm (www-data), not root — pre-v0.0.52's mode 660 owned by haproxy:haproxy was unreadable from www-data even though `docker compose exec`-via-root showed OK. Probe evolution: v0.0.34 introduced TCP-open + echo (broken at the soft-version-match stage); v0.0.35 silenced it; v0.0.37 declared the silencing honestly via expect_no_version_line: true; v0.0.43 finally restores real drift detection by querying haproxy's own runtime API. expect_zero_exit: true means socket-missing / permission-denied / hung-daemon / `show info` failure all flip the row to VerifyFailed; a deployed haproxy whose Version line doesn't contain the pinned string flips to VersionMismatch. v0.0.44 retired the v0.0.43 EOL caveat: bumped to HAProxy 3.0 LTS (active branch through ~Q2 2029 per upstream branch-status). Coordinated Dockerfile FROM + manifest pin bumped in lockstep so the v0.0.43 drift probe surfaced the EOL flag, then immediately green-flagged the upgrade. Three-way (compose ↔ Dockerfile ↔ manifest) lockstep pinning of the haproxy:3.0-alpine floating tag is queued for v0.0.45.
